name: KAMRUI Hyper H2 Mini PC (Intel Core 14450HX)
shortDescription: The KAMRUI Hyper H2 is the most powerful Intel mini PC in its price range, powered by the 10-core Intel Core 14450HX running up to 4.8GHz. With 16GB DDR4 and 512GB PCIe 4.0 storage, it handles 7B–13B local LLMs via Ollama and is one of the fastest CPU-only mini PCs for AI inference available in 2026.
useCases: Fast CPU-only LLM inference (7B–13B via Ollama)
Windows 11 Pro AI development and prototyping
Always-on private AI assistant at home or office
Edge AI inference for business deployments
pros: 10-core 14450HX — fastest Intel CPU in any mini PC at this price
Up to 4.8GHz boost — strong single-thread performance for LLM inference
PCIe 4.0 SSD — faster model loading than PCIe 3.0 alternatives
Triple 4K display support via HDMI + DP + USB-C
Upgradeable RAM up to 64GB via two SO-DIMM slots
cons: Intel UHD iGPU on HX series — no Xe acceleration for AI workloads
16GB base config limits 13B Q4 model headroom
55W TDP — warmest of the mini PC options, fan audible under load
No macOS option — Windows or Linux only
verdict: The KAMRUI Hyper H2 is the top CPU-only mini PC for local AI in this lineup. The 10-core 14450HX outperforms AMD Ryzen 4300U in multi-threaded inference by 40–60%, and the PCIe 4.0 SSD makes model loading noticeably faster. If you want the most tokens per second from a Windows mini PC without a discrete GPU, the Hyper H2 is the choice. Upgrading to 32GB RAM is highly recommended for comfortable 13B inference.

faq.question: How fast is the KAMRUI Hyper H2 for local LLMs?
faq.answer: Using Ollama on Windows 11, the 14450HX runs Llama 3.1 8B at approximately 12–20 tokens/second via CPU. This is notably faster than 4-core AMD Ryzen 4300U alternatives (5–10 t/s) thanks to the 10-core architecture. 13B Q4 models run at 6–10 tokens/second — usable for interactive chat sessions.

faq.question: Can I upgrade the RAM in the KAMRUI Hyper H2?
faq.answer: Yes. The Hyper H2 uses two standard SO-DIMM DDR4 slots and can be upgraded from the base 16GB to 32GB or 64GB. Upgrading to 32GB is strongly recommended if you plan to run 13B models regularly — it provides comfortable headroom and improves overall system responsiveness.

faq.question: How does the KAMRUI Hyper H2 compare to the GEEKOM IT12?
faq.answer: The Hyper H2 has a faster CPU (10-core 14450HX vs 8-core i5-12450H) and better single-thread performance for AI inference. The IT12 has Intel Iris Xe iGPU for OpenVINO acceleration and a better warranty (3 years vs 1 year). For raw LLM speed, choose the Hyper H2. For reliability and GPU-accelerated Windows AI features, choose the IT12.
